intent: Produce Claude Code research handover for a generic self-learning agents layer (memory + weight level) on top of Multica
did: Read concept paper + Multica + Turing Agents; clarified stack/scope; wrote RESEARCH-HANDOVER.md and lightweight project profile; fixed capture.sh empty-log bug
learned: capture.sh crashed on first capture (empty log: grep -c '' emits 0 AND exits non-zero); switched to wc -l with numeric guard

intent: Verify FireAct & AgentTuning primary-source claims for small-LLM-agent report
did: Fetched arXiv 2310.05915 (FireAct) + ACL Findings 2024 PDF of arXiv 2310.12823 (AgentTuning); extracted exact EM/MMLU tables via pdftotext
learned: User claim conflated FireAct rows: the ~77% increase to 26.2 EM is Llama2-7B fine-tuned, NOT GPT-3.5 (GPT-3.5 ft is 31.4->39.2, +25%). Verify table row->model mapping before citing.

intent: verify primary-source claims for report on fine-tuning small LLMs into agents
did: fanned out 5 research agents to verify 8 works (FireAct, AgentTuning, Lumos, ToolLLM, Gorilla, Toolformer, xLAM/APIGen, NexusRaven/Hermes) + C2 data-gen + consensus question against arXiv/GitHub/HF primary sources; flagged FireAct 77%/26.2 EM as Llama2-7B not GPT-3.5
learned: FireAct claim in the source brief mis-attributed the +77%/26.2 EM HotpotQA figure to GPT-3.5; it is actually Llama2-7B — always re-verify which table row a relative-gain number belongs to

intent: Execute RESEARCH-HANDOVER.md: go/no-go report on a generic self-learning layer for managed agents
did: Wrote docs/research/REPORT-self-learning-agents.md; fanned out 5 verification subagents over prior art (agent-tuning, memory-level, curriculum/reward, tuning infra/cost, Multica+positioning); answered all of §2.A-H, filled both §4 rubrics, gave hybrid/swappable-backend Multica recommendation + Mermaid architecture + Python library shape + annotated bibliography
learned: Two corrections surfaced and verified: (1) paper's decision rule argmax P(success|S,A) is degenerate for a curriculum -> must optimize learning-progress/regret; (2) Multica license is modified-Apache NOASSERTION (commercial-restricted), NOT MIT/Apache-2.0 as brief/prior-study claimed

intent: Fix the concept paper's gaps and produce a versioned self-learning-agent algorithm
did: Wrote docs/research/ALGORITHM-v0.1-pathway-learner.md: open-ended probabilistic pathway learning (MCTS over a growing competence graph), with a gap->fix traceability table closing all Tier 1-5 paper gaps + open-world/data/retrieval refinements; concrete Beta-Bernoulli state model, corrected frontier objective, growth rule, state-conditioned retrieval, act/learn data binding, non-stationarity+safety gates, and a memory-only v0.1 runnable subset

intent: Adversarially pressure-test PL-v0.1 algorithm to find where it breaks
did: Ran 3 independent red-team subagents (dynamics / growth+structure / reward+retrieval+safety); ~40 findings synthesized into 8 root causes + a v0.2 patch set in docs/research/ALGORITHM-v0.1-redteam.md; added a warning banner to the algorithm spec
learned: Key result: 3 INDEPENDENT pilot-killers, 2 of which fire in the memory-only v0.1 pilot itself (verifier gaming + eval-suite bootstrap gap), so v0.1 as written is unsafe to run; architecture sound, mechanisms need statistical gates + held-out/counterfactual verification + growth-provisioning coupling. Meta-principle: the system optimizes its own scoreboard -> make measurement independent of optimization. Pilot must be re-scoped: milestone 0 = growth OFF + held-out eval to prove TRUE competence moves

intent: Produce hardened PL-v0.2 folding in the red-team's 8 root-cause patches
did: Wrote docs/research/ALGORITHM-v0.2-pathway-learner.md: self-contained hardened spec organized by two principles (measurement independent of optimization; every add has an inverse); integrates statistical gate primitive, held-out+trajectory-shape+counterfactual verification, provision_suite invariant, soft reachability + merge/prune, dual mastery/drift posteriors, discounted/invalidated MCTS tree, coverage+cumulative+deployment safety, two-stage reversible promotion, re-scoped 0/1/2 pilot. Marked v0.1 superseded

intent: Rewrite the paper's method section around the hardened PL-v0.2 design
did: Wrote docs/research/PAPER-method-section.md: academic Method (replaces original paper sec 2-8), learner-agnostic with agent instantiation; formal probabilistic state (eq1), soft reachability (eq2), generalization gate (eq3), corrected learning-gain objective (eq4-5) refuting argmax P(success), evolution update + composite commit gate (eq6), two-axis promotion, act/learn architecture; preserves original engine names (Validation/Inference/Decision/Evolution); notes Related Work + Evaluation still needed

intent: Set up implementation of the pathway-learner inside turing-agents repo
did: Wrote docs/mdlp/IMPLEMENTATION.md (M0-M2 build guide, opt-in Layer 3, pytest verifier) + copied design specs to docs/mdlp/design/; populated tasks/BOARD.md with 12 staged MDLP tasks assigned to pathway-builder/eval-harness-builder/@you/agent-smith
learned: Honored L-010: built as opt-in Layer 3 package (own deps) reading canonical file logs, app/ stays pure-stdlib; NOT in app/. Also found+fixed a real bug in their scripts/task.sh (octal parse error at IDs >= 008; fixed with 10# base-10 forcing)

intent: Write Related Work + Evaluation protocol and assemble the full paper
did: Wrote docs/research/PAPER.md: full draft (Abstract, Intro, Related Work across 6 themes with verified citations, integrated Method, design-only pre-registered Evaluation protocol with baselines/ablations/metric-definitions/go-no-go, Discussion, Limitations, Conclusion, References). Framed honestly as a framework+protocol paper with NO results; drops the original's unmethodized +18%/+23%
learned: Evaluation is pre-registered (success criteria fixed in advance) to avoid the measurement-dependence the framework itself warns against; each ablation maps to a principle + the red-team failure it guards; classic education citations (BKT/DKT/IRT) flagged to confirm to house style at submission

intent: Design the paper's 5-store hybrid as a pluggable Python data-layer library
did: Wrote docs/mdlp/DATA-LAYER.md (mirrored to research repo): 6 store roles (SQL/Mongo/Vector/Graph/Redis + artifact) as ports&adapters; two tiers (embedded zero-infra default / full 5-store); per-role config; schemas; rebuild-from-truth discipline; DI into the algorithm; optional-extras deps; extractable as standalone pathway-stores lib
learned: Reconciled the earlier 'don't stand up 5 stores' discipline with the user wanting all 5: make them pluggable interfaces, embedded by default, flip to heavy DBs per-role under the evidence gate; SQL truth canonical + others rebuildable projections is what makes it swappable

intent: Implement the full 5-store tier as code in the mdlp/stores library
did: Built mdlp/ package in turing-agents: ports.py (6 store protocols), schemas.py, config.py (per-role backend select + url/env/toml), lazy-loading factory (__init__), rebuild.py (truth->projection replay), pyproject with per-backend extras, README, 7 passing contract tests; + 6 full-tier DB adapters via 6 parallel subagents: Postgres(truth), Mongo(state), Qdrant(vector), Neo4j(graph), Redis(cache), S3/MinIO(artifact)
learned: Package compiles + imports with ZERO db drivers (lazy import per backend); selecting a full backend without its driver raises a helpful 'pip install mdlp[x]' ImportError. NOT runtime-verified vs live DBs (drivers absent here). Embedded tier + docker-compose integration tests are the next pieces

intent: Research the landscape of other self-learning AI agents
did: Ran the deep-research workflow (106 agents, 24 primary sources, 25/25 claims verified 3-0); synthesized docs/research/LANDSCAPE-self-learning-agents.md — two mechanism classes (memory-level established; weight-level Agentic RL) + the 2025-26 self-modification/self-evolution frontier (DGM, self-evolving surveys, Absolute Zero/R-Zero/ALAS/EigenData), continuous-vs-offline distinction, and MDLP positioning
learned: HEADLINE: the agentic-RL survey (2509.02547) itself names MDLP's exact locus — a learned meta-policy that picks the FORM of learning per task by expected gain — as the open frontier ('handcrafted and static'); no surveyed system gates next-action selection by a held-out verifier. White space is real but MDLP unproven. Caveat: DGM/EigenData numbers are self-reported; survey's 'R-Zero=MCTS' label is wrong (it's Challenger-Solver self-play) — don't repeat

intent: Read-only review of the mdlp reference implementation (turing-agents)
did: Wrote docs/research/IMPLEMENTATION-REVIEW.md: reviewed state/eval/decision/loop/memory/domain/bench + adapters/learning,runner + M0.md against v0.2 + the red-team; NO changes to turing-agents (read-only per the scope boundary)
learned: Verdict: faithful, honest, well-tested v0.2 machinery BUT every result so far is harness-defined. C1: M0 is a wiring proof not a learning result (domain.py hardcodes held_rate=base+gain*n; +0.487 is a baked constant). C2: DecisionEngine (the differentiator) isn't in the e2e run (round-robin hardcoded). C3: verifier shape-check is a self-declared-flag stub; only the counterfactual probe is real. Genuine learning correctly gated on the unwired live runner

intent: Write an improvement note for the turing-agents mdlp implementation, linked to the review
did: Wrote docs/research/IMPROVEMENT-NOTE-turing-agents.md (prioritized P0-P3 hand-off: P0 live B7 with DecisionEngine in the loop; P1 honest relabel + real shape-analysis + same-sample gate; P2 C0 re-red-team + growth-provisioning; P3 self-mod axis/task-gen/learned-pi) and cross-linked it bidirectionally with IMPLEMENTATION-REVIEW.md; kept in MDLP repo since turing-agents is managed separately

intent: Integrate the Tutor layer into v0.2 + distill brainstorm takeaways
did: Added §13 'Tutor layer' to ALGORITHM-v0.2: generic embedded Tutor (the DecisionEngine elevated, learner-agnostic) + pluggable TeacherAdapter (pedagogy, selected by held-out gain) + LearnerAdapter (human/agent); §13.1 info-gain mode (argmax E[ΔH], Bayesian active learning) as the bias-free objective. Additive — no §1-12 change. Mermaid + integration map

intent: Create NEXT-STEPS backlog + the C1 human-learning-verifier design
did: Wrote docs/research/NEXT-STEPS.md (A/B/C/D backlog from the brainstorm, prioritized) and docs/research/HUMAN-LEARNING-VERIFIER.md (C1: held-out assessment + IRT/guessing + behavioural-signal likelihood + predictive-validity admission; the agent hardening maps over verbatim — generalization gate=teaching-to-test, decay=cramming, counterfactual=isomorphic item; go/no-go = verifiable domains first; info-gain selection forced by sparse human data)

intent: Integrate a calibration layer into v0.2 (§14)
did: Added §14 'calibration layer' to ALGORITHM-v0.2 (additive): per-band Calibrator pairs prediction p̂ with realized held-out outcome, monitors ECE/Brier, applies isotonic point-recalibration (g_band) + uncertainty recalibration via n_eff deflation c_band (the mirror of the n_min floor) so SE becomes honest; every existing SE-keyed gate (significant/Thompson/info-gain/commit) inherits it unchanged; ECE>τ_cal is a 5th circuit-breaker trigger; conformal prediction as the distribution-free option. Updated NEXT-STEPS A3

intent: Consolidate brainstorm learnings into an integration register + v0.2 §15 re-visiting loop
did: Wrote docs/research/ALGORITHM-INTEGRATIONS.md (the single source-of-truth register: A-G groups, every learning → integration point, status ✅/🆕/🔭) and added ALGORITHM-v0.2 §15 'Re-visiting data: the surprise-gated learning loop' (revisit(D) as a first-class action; insight=prediction error; info-gain trigger + surprise-decay/budget stop; two-level MCTS for deterministic domains; negative-evidence-as-gain; loops-shorten-as-you-assimilate; generative path storage). NEXT-STEPS points to the register + A4 logged
